title: A2 — Rename `scope:` to `relevance_scope:` in fabric-archive SKILL.md across three mirrors
description: Rename all `scope:` literals to `relevance_scope:` in fabric-archive SKILL.md Phase 2 MCP call, worked examples, and Hard Rules; mirror to .claude/skills + .codex/skills (byte-identical). Update protected tokens registry from `scope` to `relevance_scope` (or add `relevance_scope` while keeping `scope` if the old token is still used elsewhere — verify via lint).

files.target: Phase 2 fab_extract_knowledge call (L322-338) + Phase 1.5 narrative + Hard Rules + worked examples
files.change: Replace `scope:` → `relevance_scope:` in all MCP call payloads, narrative prose, worked example JSON blocks; preserve fab_extract_knowledge token + MUST/NEVER anchors
Byte-identical copy of updated template (overwrite via fab init or hand-mirror)
Byte-identical copy of updated template (overwrite via fab init or hand-mirror)
Add `relevance_scope` to the protected token registry; verify whether `scope` (bare) was previously listed and remove if so
Update expected token set to include relevance_scope

implementation: Replace `scope: narrow` / `scope: broad` / `scope=narrow|broad` text → `relevance_scope: ...`; preserve the relevance_paths line untouched (already correctly named)
Verify Phase 2 worked-example JSON blocks use relevance_scope in MCP call arguments (matches the schema extension in TASK-001)
Copy modified template to .claude/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md and .codex/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md (use `diff -q` to confirm byte-identity)
Update protected-tokens.ts + lint test expectations; run scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts to verify no token regression
Run install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts to verify three-mirror parity

reference.pattern: Three-mirror byte-identity; protected token registry sync
reference.examples: Follow the SKILL_DESTINATIONS fan-out pattern; lint-protected-tokens.ts walks templates/ and validates registry presence verbatim

criteria: fab_extract_knowledge / MUST / NEVER / .fabric/knowledge/ tokens still present verbatim in all three files

rationale.chosen_approach: Simple rename + three-mirror copy; depends on TASK-001 because schema must accept the field name before SKILL.md references it
alternatives_considered: Keep `scope:` as alias in schema (backwards-compat with old SKILL.md text)
Rename in template only and let next fab init propagate
decision_factors: Three-mirror byte-identity is a HARD constraint
Protected token registry must stay in sync with template surface
rationale.tradeoffs: Existing fabric-archive users running stale mirrors will see field-name mismatch until they re-run fab init; mitigated by doctor lint #26 narrowing imported entries

manual_checks: diff -q the three mirror files pairwise
Open template SKILL.md and visually confirm no orphan `scope:` strings outside relevance_scope context
success_metrics: 0 occurrences of bare `scope:` in all three mirrors
